previous_core_stack: Layer 5 ATR14 x 2.5 trailing stop + time stop 15 days
updated_core_stack: Layer 5 ATR14 x 2.5 trailing stop + provisional time stop 12 days
what_changed: Explicit `close > ema50` had already been classified as redundant/non-binding and is no longer part of the active core decision.
`return_20d > 0` is now removed from the core stack on a provisional basis after cycle 2.
Layer 5 is now provisionally revised from time stop 15 days to 12 days while keeping ATR14 x 2.5 and trailing logic unchanged.
No changes were made to Layer 3 or Layer 4.

reasoning: Cycle 2 candidate `Phase 1 + Layer 1 only` improved out-of-sample average return per trade from -0.0909 to 0.1342.
Cycle 2 candidate improved out-of-sample profit factor from 0.9629 to 1.0270.
Cycle 2 candidate reduced OOS degradation vs in-sample from 114.819 to 69.0498.
These improvements established the provisional entry simplification baseline before the shorter Layer 5 time stop validation was applied.
why_return20d_positive_was_provisionally.provisional_only_note: The drop is not final permanent freeze because final holdout remains negative and drawdown remains too high.

reasoning: The shorter time stop validation selected 12 days as the best Layer 5 variant against the 15-day control.
On final holdout, 12 days improved average return per trade from -0.4595 to 0.0261, profit factor from 0.8420 to 0.9932, and max drawdown from 34.4658% to 28.5468%.
The revision keeps ATR14 x 2.5 and trailing logic unchanged, so the policy change remains narrow and formally scoped.
why_layer5_was_provisionally_revised.provisional_only_note: The time stop revision is provisional, not final permanent freeze, because holdout profitability is still borderline and drawdown remains above the operational target.

reasons: Final holdout average_return_per_trade is only 0.0261, so profitability is still borderline rather than convincingly positive.
Final holdout profit factor remains below 1.0 at 0.9932.
Final holdout max drawdown remains high at 28.5468%.
The provisional core stack is stronger than before, but it still has not converted the full validation path into a paper-trading candidate.

why_selected: OOS resilience and holdout damage both improved materially after the provisional core stack update, so the narrowest remaining failure is holdout profitability that is still only borderline.
Drawdown is still high, but the latest Layer 5 revision already reduced it materially, so the next planning cycle should stay focused on profitability recovery first.
Operational readiness is still not passed, but that remains downstream of economic edge quality rather than the first redesign target itself.

official_next_action.note: The next planning cycle should focus only on holdout profitability borderline for the updated provisional core stack, without reopening Layer 4, ATR multiplier, trailing logic, or Layer 3 redesign.
